source_citation: OurWorldinData.org/child-mortality; Data source: Gapminder (2015); UN Inter-agency Group for Child Mortality Estimation (2025)

y_axis.title: Child mortality rate (%)
y_axis.title_verbatim: Estimated share of newborns who die before age 5.

chart_subtitle: Estimated share of newborns who die before age 5.

extraction_method.phase_3_recipe: Line/curve trace (SKILL.md §3): HSV color mask per series + per-column median row of mask after connected-component filtering. CC pipeline dilates the binary mask with a 2x2 kernel before component labeling to merge anti-alias-fragmented runs, then keeps components >=20 px. Brazil and Sweden share hue range (102-113); separated by V (Brazil dark V<=130, Sweden lighter V>=130).
extraction_method.phase_4_postproc: Linear interpolation of gaps <=5 years. Post-1945 spike removal via two passes: (a) 7-year median filter for single-year spikes; (b) bilateral monotonic-decline detector for sustained cross-line contamination runs (collapsed Sweden 1958-1991 contamination cluster).
extraction_method.phase_4_overlay_check: Per-extracted-point pixel overlay onto image.png in overlay.png; per-series mean error: Ghana 0.0px, India 1.4px, Brazil 0.0px, US 0.0px, UK 0.0px, France 0.6px, Sweden 1.7px (max 134px on Sweden 1958-1991 region where pixel evidence is absent and values are despike interpolations).

known_limitations: France ends at 1979 and Sweden at 2010: their distinct color pixels are overwritten by adjacent series in the low-percent range (<1%) where lines visually merge. OWID's underlying data goes to 2023.
Early Sweden (1751-1830) is genuinely noisy historical data with ~10pp year-to-year swings; per-year sampling captures most peaks but may alias rapid spikes <1 px column wide.

Sustained extraction artifacts in Sweden over 1958-1991 (cross-line contamination from US/UK overlapping color hue) were despiked using monotonic-decline interpolation; these years should be interpreted as model-fit rather than direct pixel reads.
notes: OWID-style chart with line-endpoint country labels at right and inset axis-end labels (1751 and 2023 sit inside the data range). X-axis calibration uses interior-tick least-squares (excluding 1751 and 2023 inset labels). Y-axis calibration is a 6-tick fit with residuals < 0.4 px.
